headline: Sabah Shark Fin Trade Worth RM48M Despite 2014 Ban
context: A 2025 TRAFFIC investigation documented RM48 million in shark fin exports from Sabah in 2024 despite the state's 2014 ban on shark finning in its waters.

cards.big: Sabah banned shark finning in 2014 — the first state in Malaysia.
cards.sub: A decade later, RM48M in shark fins still leave Sabah annually through Sandakan and Tawau ports.

cards.big: Sabah Parks has 12 marine enforcement officers for 3,500km of coastline.
cards.sub: That is one officer per 292km — equivalent to one police officer for all of Penang Island.

cards.big: Shark-based tourism at Sipadan generates RM32M annually.
cards.sub: Live sharks are worth more than dead ones, but the economic case has not translated to enforcement funding.

cards.big: 78% of confiscated fins in 2024 came from endangered species.
cards.sub: Including scalloped hammerhead and oceanic whitetip — both CITES Appendix II listed.

cards.big: A ban without enforcement is a suggestion. Sharks cannot tell the difference.
Marine laws mean nothing without patrol boats and officers. Sabah's shark ban needs a budget, not another anniversary.
